zh.lifeSummary: 當Lady Sybil de Lea於1283年出生在英國爾時，她的父親Sir William De Lea Baron of Kendall46歲，母親Clemence de Banastre44歲。她於大約1305年與Richard de Houghton在英國英格蘭爾莫利頓結婚。他們至少是2個兒子和4個女兒的父母。她去世於1330年在英國爾莫利頓享年47歲，埋葬在英國。

zh-hans.lifeSummary: 当Lady Sybil de Lea于1283年出生在英国爾时，她的父亲Sir William De Lea Baron of Kendall46岁，母亲Clemence de Banastre44岁。她于大约1305年与Richard de Houghton在英国英格兰爾莫利頓结婚。他们至少是2个儿子和4个女儿的父母。她去世于1330年在英国爾莫利頓享年47岁，埋葬在英国。

nameMeanings.name: de
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Indian Dilip, Anirban, Gopal, Alok, Anindya, Arunava, Asit, Asok, Dibyendu, Goutam, Indra, Indranil.

variants.name: Dey
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Indian Ashit, Indu, Naina, Partha, Soumya, Ajit, Anjan, Anjana, Anup, Arjun, Arun, Ashim.
English, Scottish, and Welsh: variant of   Day  .
German: topographic name from a field name in North Rhine-Westphalia, denoting a sizeable piece of land, from Middle Low German dē ‘thigh’ (referring to the shape of the property).

nameMeanings.name: Lea
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: Chinese: variant Romanization of the surnames 李 and 利, see   Li   1 and 3.

variants.name: Lee
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Chinese Young, Sang, Jae, Jong, Jung, Sung, Yong, Kyung, Seung, Dong, Kwang, Myung.

English: topographic name for someone who lived near a meadow or a patch of arable land, Middle English lee, lea, from Old English lēa, dative case (used after a preposition) of lēah, which originally meant ‘wood or glade’.

English: habitational name from any of the many places in England named with Old English lēah ‘wood, glade’, including Lee in Buckinghamshire, Essex, Hampshire, Kent, and Shropshire, and Lea in Cheshire, Derbyshire, Herefordshire, Lancashire, Lincolnshire, and Wiltshire.

nameMeanings.name: Sybil
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.
meanings: From the name (Greek Sibylla or Sybilla, with confusion over the vowels from an early period) of a class of ancient prophetesses inspired by Apollo. According to medieval theology, they were pagans denied the knowledge of Christ but blessed by God with some insight into things to come and accordingly admitted to heaven. It was thus regarded as a respectable name to be borne by Christians. This name was introduced to England by the Normans and enjoyed considerable popularity in the Middle Ages.

variants.name: Sybilla
meanings: From the name (Greek Sibylla or Sybilla, with confusion over the vowels from an early period) of a class of ancient prophetesses inspired by Apollo. It was thus regarded as a respectable name to be borne by Christians. This name was introduced to England by the Normans and enjoyed considerable popularity in the Middle Ages.

historicEvents.title: Jews Expelled From England
historicEvents.description: After years of anti-Semitic behavior, King Edward I expelled Jews from England in 1290.

historicEvents.title: Famine Strikes Europe
historicEvents.description: A famine caused by cold winters and wet summers across Europe caused millions to die due to starvation. Desperation from hunger caused high levels of crime, stampedes in bread lines in London, infanticide, and even cannibalism throughout Europe.

historicEvents.title: First War of Scottish Independence
historicEvents.description: The First War of Scottish Independence began after England invaded Scotland in 1296. The Treaty of Edinburgh was signed in 1328 which declared peace between the two kingdoms and brought the war to an end.

en.lifeSummary: When Lady Sybil de Lea was born in 1283, in Cheshire, England, her father, Sir William De Lea Baron of Kendall, was 46 and her mother, Clemence de Banastre, was 44. She married Richard de Houghton about 1305, in Mollington, Cheshire, England, United Kingdom. They were the parents of at least 2 sons and 4 daughters. She died in 1330, in Mollington, Cheshire, England, at the age of 47, and was buried in England.

fr.lifeSummary: Lorsque Lady Sybil de Lea est née en 1283, à Cheshire, Angleterre, son père, Sir William De Lea Baron of Kendall, avait 46 ans et sa mère, Clemence de Banastre, avait 44 ans. Elle a épousé Richard de Houghton environ 1305, à Mollington, Cheshire, Angleterre, Royaume-Uni. Ils ont eu au moins 2 fils et 4 filles. Elle est décédée en 1330, à Mollington, Cheshire, Angleterre, à l’âge de 47 ans, et a été enterrée à Angleterre.

de.lifeSummary: Als Lady Sybil de Lea 1283 in Cheshire, England geboren wurde, war ihr Vater Sir William De Lea Baron of Kendall 46 und ihre Mutter Clemence de Banastre 44. Sie hat um 1305 in Mollington, Cheshire, England, United Kingdom Richard de Houghton geheiratet. Sie hatten mindestens 2 Söhne und 4 Töchter. Sie starb 1330 im Alter von 47 Jahren in Mollington, Cheshire, England und wurde in England begraben.

it.lifeSummary: Quando Lady Sybil de Lea è nata il nel 1283 nel Cheshire, England, sua padre, Sir William De Lea Baron of Kendall, aveva 46 anni e sua madre, Clemence de Banastre, aveva 44 anni. Lei sposato Richard de Houghton circa 1305, nel Mollington, Cheshire, Inghilterra, Regno Unito. Erano genitori di almeno 2 figli e 4 figlie. Morì nel 1330, nel Mollington, Cheshire, England, all'età di 47 anni, e fu sepolta a England.

ja.lifeSummary: Lady Sybil de Leaが1283年にイギリスチェシュアイアで生まれた時，父Sir William De Lea Baron of Kendallは46歳，母Clemence de Banastreは44歳であった。約1305年にイギリスイギリスチェシュアイアモリントンでRichard de Houghtonと結婚した。彼らは少なくとも2人の息子と4人の娘の親であった。彼女は1330年にイギリスチェシュアイアモリントンで47歳で死去し，イギリスにて埋葬された。

ko.lifeSummary: Lady Sybil de Lea(이)가 1283년 영국 체실르에서 태어났을 때, 그녀의 아버지 Sir William De Lea Baron of Kendall(은)는 46세였고, 그녀의 어머니 Clemence de Banastre(은)는 44세였다. 그녀는 대략 1305년 영국 잉글랜드 체실르 모링턴에서 Richard de Houghton(와)과 결혼했다. 그들은 적어도 두 명의 아들과 네 명의 딸을 두었다. 그녀는 1330년 영국 체실르 모링턴에서 47세의 나이로 사망했으며 영국에 묻혔다.

pt.Lea Hall, Preston, Lancashire, England: Lea Hall, Lancashire, Inglaterra, United Kingdom
pt.lifeSummary: Quando Lady Sybil de Lea nasceu em 1283, em Cheshire, Inglaterra, seu pai, Sir William De Lea Baron of Kendall, tinha 46 anos e sua mãe, Clemence de Banastre, tinha 44 anos. Ela casou-se com Richard de Houghton aproximadamente 1305, em Mollington, Cheshire, Inglaterra, United Kingdom. Eles tiveram pelo menos 2 filhos e 4 filhas. Ela faleceu em 1330, em Mollington, Cheshire, Inglaterra, com 47 anos, e foi sepultada em Inglaterra.

ro.Lea Hall, Preston, Lancashire, England: Lea Hall, Lancashire, Anglia, United Kingdom

ru.lifeSummary: Когда Lady Sybil de Lea родилась в 1283 г. в местности чешир, Англия, её отцу по имени Sir William De Lea Baron of Kendall было 46 лет, а матери, которую звали Clemence de Banastre, 44. Она вышла замуж за человека по имени Richard de Houghton около 1305 г. в местности моллингтон, чешир, Англия, Великобритания. У них было по крайней мере 2 сына и 4 дочери. Она умерла в 1330 г. в местности моллингтон, чешир, Англия в возрасте 47 лет и была похоронена в местности Англия.

Se casó con Richard de Houghton aproximadamente 1305, en Mollington, Cheshire, Inglaterra, Reino Unido. Fueron padres de por lo menos 2 hijos y 4 hijas. Murió en 1330, en Mollington, Cheshire, Inglaterra, a la edad de 47 años, y fue sepultada en Inglaterra.
